_comment: ILLUSTRATIVE sample — a SYNTHETIC paper and INVENTED numbers, shown only to teach the exact shape resolve_analysis must return. Not a real paper; do not cite. Delete the _comment key in real output (the engine ignores it). See ../examples/worked-example.md for the end-to-end walkthrough and ../references/ara-schema.md for the field contract.
overview: A latent world model is learned from interaction and used for imagination-based planning, decoupling representation learning from control so the policy can be optimized inside the model rather than on the environment.

observations.statement: Model-free control is sample-inefficient on long-horizon manipulation.
observations.evidence: Reported environment-step counts in prior work are large.

gaps.statement: Prior latent models drift over long rollouts.
gaps.why_fail: One-step reconstruction loss does not penalize compounding error.

insight.statement: Predicting value-equivalent quantities, not pixels, keeps long imagined rollouts useful for planning.
insight.enables: Stable long-horizon imagination that the planner can trust.

assumptions: The reward is observable during training.
The latent dynamics are approximately Markov.

claims.title: Sample-efficient planning
claims.statement: The latent world model attains an 87.3% task success rate, a 12.4-point improvement over the strongest model-free baseline.
claims.falsification: Success rate is not above the baseline under matched interaction budget.
claims.interpretation: Imagination-based planning closes most of the sample-efficiency gap.

claims.title: Rollout stability
claims.statement: Value-equivalent prediction reduces compounding rollout error versus pixel reconstruction.
claims.falsification: Ablating value-equivalence does not increase rollout error.
claims.interpretation: The value-equivalence term is the load-bearing piece for long rollouts.

concepts.name: latent dynamics model
concepts.definition: A learned transition function over a compact latent state.
concepts.interpretation: A compressed simulator of the environment.
concepts.boundary: Holds only within the training distribution of dynamics.

concepts.name: imagination rollout
concepts.definition: A trajectory generated entirely inside the latent model.
concepts.interpretation: Planning without touching the real environment.
concepts.boundary: Quality degrades past the horizon the model was trained to.

concepts.name: value-equivalent prediction
concepts.definition: Training the model to preserve quantities relevant to value, not raw observations.
concepts.interpretation: Predict what matters for the decision, not the pixels.
concepts.boundary: Requires a reward signal at training time.

concepts.name: world model
concepts.definition: A learned generative model of environment dynamics used for planning.
concepts.interpretation: An internal simulator the agent plans inside.
concepts.boundary: Accurate only where the training data covered the dynamics.

concepts.name: policy distillation
concepts.definition: Training a reactive policy to imitate the planner's chosen actions.
concepts.interpretation: Compresses expensive planning into a fast reactive policy.
concepts.boundary: Bounded by the planner's own action quality.

experiments.title: Sample-efficiency comparison

setup.system: Matched interaction budget vs a model-free baseline

procedure: Train both methods under the same environment-step budget.
Evaluate mean task success rate across the six tasks.
experiments.expected: Higher success rate than the model-free baseline under a matched budget.

experiments.title: Value-equivalence ablation

setup.model: Latent world model with the value-equivalence loss removed

procedure: Measure long-horizon rollout error and downstream success.
experiments.expected: Rollout error rises and downstream success drops relative to the full model.

experiments.title: Imagination-horizon sweep

setup.model: Latent world model at varying imagined horizons

procedure: Measure rollout error and downstream success at each horizon.
experiments.expected: Success peaks near the trained horizon and degrades beyond it.

related_work.what_changed: Adds a value-equivalent objective on top of latent-dynamics planning.
related_work.why: Reconstruction-based latents waste capacity on detail irrelevant to control.

related_work.what_changed: Couples value-equivalence with long imagined rollouts rather than short ones.
related_work.why: Short rollouts under-use the model's predictive horizon.

architecture: Encoder -> Latent Dynamics -> Reward/Value Heads -> Imagination Planner -> Policy. The encoder maps observations to z; the dynamics roll z forward; the planner optimizes actions inside the model; the policy is distilled from planned actions.
algorithm:
Latent rollout objective (illustrative):
$$ \mathcal{L} = \sum_{t} \big\| v_\phi(z_t) - \hat{v}_t \big\|^2 + \lambda \, \mathrm{KL}\!\big(q(z_t\mid o_t)\,\|\,p(z_t\mid z_{t-1},a_{t-1})\big) $$
constraints:
# Constraints

- Requires a reward signal during training.
- Imagined rollouts degrade past the trained horizon.

heuristics.desc: Warm-start the planner from the policy's action.
heuristics.rationale: The distilled policy is already a good action prior, so planning converges faster.
heuristics.sensitivity: Low — affects planner iterations, not final quality.
heuristics.bounds: Skip when the policy is untrained.

heuristics.desc: Cap imagined horizon to the model's trained rollout length.
heuristics.rationale: Beyond the trained horizon the latent dynamics leave their valid regime.
heuristics.sensitivity: High — too long a horizon reintroduces compounding error.

configs_training.name: optimizer
configs_training.rationale: Standard adaptive optimizer for latent-model training.

configs_training.name: lr
configs_training.rationale: Stable for the joint encoder/dynamics objective.

configs_training.name: batch_size
configs_training.rationale: Fits single-GPU memory at the chosen latent size.

configs_training.name: imagination_horizon
configs_training.rationale: Longest horizon the dynamics stay reliable for.

configs_model.name: latent_dim
configs_model.rationale: Enough capacity to encode task-relevant state without overfitting.

configs_model.name: params_million
configs_model.rationale: Total trainable parameters at the chosen widths.

execution_stub:
import torch


def imagine(z0, policy, dynamics, horizon):
    z, traj = z0, []
    for _ in range(horizon):
        a = policy(z)
        z = dynamics(z, a)
        traj.append((z, a))
    return traj

innovations.name: Value-equivalent latent objective for long rollouts.
Planner warm-started from a distilled policy.

exploration_tree.title: Why do long imagined rollouts drift?
exploration_tree.description: Diagnose what makes multi-step latent rollouts diverge from real dynamics.

exploration_tree.title: Pixel-reconstruction latents
exploration_tree.failure_mode: Capacity is spent on control-irrelevant detail; long rollouts drift.

exploration_tree.title: Value-equivalence vs reconstruction rollout error
exploration_tree.result: Value-equivalent latents show lower long-horizon drift.

exploration_tree.title: Horizon-50 rollouts
exploration_tree.failure_mode: Beyond ~15 steps the dynamics leave their trained regime and error compounds.
exploration_tree.lesson: Cap the imagined horizon to the trained rollout length.

exploration_tree.title: Planner warm-start ablation
exploration_tree.result: Warm-starting the planner from the distilled policy speeds convergence.

exploration_tree.title: From short to long imagined rollouts
exploration_tree.trigger: Short rollouts under-use the model's predictive horizon.

evidence_tables.name: main_results
evidence_tables.caption: Mean success rate (%), matched interaction budget.

math_intuition: Like rehearsing a route in your head: you only need to remember the turns that change the outcome, not every shopfront along the way.
math_toy_example: If two latent states lead to the same value, the model is free to merge them — it only must preserve what changes the decision.

loss_highlight.direction: The value term keeps long imagined rollouts decision-relevant.
loss_highlight.mechanism: The KL term ties the posterior to the learned prior so the latent stays predictable.
loss_highlight.baseline: Dropping the value term is what lets rollout error compound, unlike pixel reconstruction.

trend: Recent model-based methods move from reconstructing observations toward predicting decision-relevant quantities; this paper sits on that trajectory.
